sections.translation:
## Control Room Monitoring

The **CONTROL** outputs feature **1/8" female TRS (mini-jack)** connectors for speakers and headphones. The speaker output operates at a nominal level of **0 dBu**, designed for connection to active monitors.\
In the console's MONITOR section, you'll find the controls for signal routing and level adjustment for both speakers and headphones.

<Note>
  The **Control Speaker** output is automatically muted when **Talk-back** is engaged, preventing feedback with the announcer microphone.
</Note>

---
sections.title: Monitoreo en Cabina de Control

sections.translation: ## Studio Monitoring The console delivers monitoring signals to the Studio via **STUDIO MONITOR OUT**, with an RJ45 connector. The connection is made using **straight-through STP RJ45-RJ45 cable** to the optional **Solidyne Studiobox HD3/HD5** monitor units. This output **must not be connected** using standard audio output RJ45 cables.\ It is **not compatible** with the **StudioHub** standard. Features: - Studio speakers **automatically mute** when microphones are opened. - The headphone signal **is never muted**. It always receives the console-assigned signal and the talkback microphone audio. For details, see the **Studiobox HD3 / HD5 Monitoring Modules** section.
sections.title: Monitoreo en el Estudio

sections.translation:
## Single-Booth Monitoring

When the console is used in an integrated or single-booth studio, it is advisable to connect the speakers to the **STUDIO MONITOR** output.

<Warning>
  The CONTROL speaker output **is not muted when microphones are activated**, creating a risk of feedback if microphones and speakers are in the same room.
</Warning>

---
sections.title: Monitoreo en cabina única

sections.translation: ## Optional Studiobox Connection The console includes a **Y-cable adapter** (RJ45 to dual TRS jack) for connecting generic headphone distribution amplifiers and powered monitors. - The **STUDIO MONITOR** output delivers fixed-level signals - When not using Studiobox, speaker level is adjusted from the monitors' own controls - A dual-input headphone distribution amplifier may be used to control monitor volume from the console - Input A → headphone signal - Input B → speaker signal Speaker volume control is assigned to Input B, allowing a single unit to control both studio headphones and monitors.
sections.title: Conexión sin opcional Studiobox

sections.translation: ### Studio Monitor Out | # | Signal | | :-: | ------------------------------- | | 1 | Headphones Left (\+4 dBu) | | 2 | Headphones Right | | 3 | GND | | 4 | ON-AIR (\+14 V) | | 5 | Talkback to Control Room | | 6 | VCC \+ 14 V | | 7 | Speakers Left (\+4 dBu) | | 8 | Speakers Right | Chassis = GND
sections.title: Studio Monitor Out
